rows.notes: Amounts are well aligned with the vertical ruling lines; pence reads '3' with a clear halfpenny loop (interpreted as 0.5). The left-hand text is legible but the first word appears to be a Latin form (spelling 'Arrearagia' uncertain) — transcription follows the visible letters rather than a normalized spelling.

Centered header with no amounts. Handwriting for 'Mixed Rents Receivable' is fairly clear though the middle word (Rents) and final word have cursive flourishes; identified as a section heading because no numbers align to the columns.

Numeric columns are crisp and clearly aligned: 210 | 18 | 9. Place name reads like 'Kidlington' (K may be ambiguous but letters fit common parish name); 'Augmentation 1 year' is clear. Minor uncertainty only in initial letter of place name.

Amounts read clearly as 115 | 2 | 3 with a three-quarter sign (interpreted as 0.75). The place name is somewhat uncertain in the manuscript; I read it as 'Long Wittenham' which fits the letter shapes and local parishes, but alternate readings of the place name are possible due to cursive ligatures.

rows.description: South Newington Do: 1 year to Mich. 1830
rows.notes: Numeric columns read 195 | 8 | 9 with a small 'q' or quarter sign after the pence (interpreted as 0.25). The description reads 'South Newington Do: 1 year to Mich. 1830' — the abbreviation 'Do:' (ditto) and the date 'Mich. 1830' are legible.
Amounts clearly read 240 | 2 | 5. Parenthetical note '(Land Tax deducted)' is legible. Alignment with ruling lines is unambiguous.
Amounts read clearly as 50 | 6 | 5 with a halfpenny loop after the pence (0.5). 'Yarnton Rectory' and '1 year' are written clearly in the description.

Numeric columns read as 28 | 10 | 7. The place name looks like 'Chase Hile' or 'Chase Hill' — I transcribed 'Chase Hill' as the most plausible reading, but the final letter of the first word is somewhat indistinct in the cursive.
Amounts read 9 | 7 | 0 with a small quarter sign after the zero pence (interpreted as 0.25). 'Clifton Ferry 1 year' transcription is clear in the description.

Numeric columns read 9 | 10 | 3 with a halfpenny after the pence (0.5). The place-name / term is uncertain in the handwriting — appears as 'Garsington Clinkard' or similar; I selected a likely parish-like rendering but alternate spellings are possible.
Amounts read clearly as 47 | 5 | 2. The description begins with 'Do:' (ditto) indicating same principal heading; 'Crooke 5 years' is legible. Alignment with columns is unambiguous.

Final ruled total line reads 1078 | 7 | 1 with a small quarter after the pence (interpreted as 0.25). The total is aligned under the pounds/shillings/pence columns and the rule beneath confirms it is a summation line.
